children.heading: Policies relating to restricted and unrestricted reporting of sexual assault and sexual harassment
children.label: a Policies relating to restricted and unrestricted reporting of sexual assault and sexual harassment

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall develop and implement policies, regulations, personnel training, and workforce education to establish and provide information about restricted reports and unrestricted reports of allegations of sexual assault and sexual harassment within the Agency in accordance with this subsection.

children.heading: Workforce education
children.label: 2 Workforce education
children.text: Workforce education developed under paragraph (1) shall be designed to clearly inform Agency employees of the differences between restricted and unrestricted reporting of allegations of sexual assault and sexual harassment, and which individual or office within the Agency is responsible for receiving each type of report.

children.heading: Relationship to the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office
children.label: 3 Relationship to the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office
A
children.text: encourage Agency employees to make restricted or unrestricted reports of sexual assault and sexual harassment to the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office;

children.label: B
children.text: encourage Agency employees to use the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office as the primary point of contact and entry point for Agency employees to make restricted or unrestricted reports of sexual assault and sexual harassment;

children.label: C
children.text: encourage Agency employees to seek the victim advocacy services of the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office after reporting an allegation of sexual assault or sexual harassment, to the extent consistent with the victim’s election; and

children.label: D
children.text: encourage Agency employees and individuals who receive disclosures of sexual assault and sexual harassment to provide the report to, and receive guidance from, the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office.

children.heading: Election
children.label: b Election
children.text: Any person making a report containing an allegation of a sexual assault or sexual harassment shall elect whether to make a restricted report or an unrestricted report. Once an election is made to make an unrestricted report, such election may not be changed.

children.heading: Assistance
children.label: 1 Assistance
children.text: A person who elects to make an unrestricted report containing an allegation of sexual assault or sexual harassment may seek the assistance of another employee of the Agency with taking the action required under paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: A list of physicians and mental health care providers (including from the private sector, as applicable) who have experience with the physical and mental health care needs of the Agency workforce.

children.label: B
children.text: A list of chaplains and religious counselors who have experience with the needs of the Agency workforce, including information regarding access to the Chaplain Corps established under .section 3527 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: Information regarding how to select and retain private attorneys who have experience with the legal needs of the Agency workforce, including detailed information on the process for the appropriate sharing of information with retained private attorneys.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 3 Rule of construction
children.text: The inclusion of any person on a list maintained or made available pursuant to subsection (c)(2) shall not be construed as an endorsement of such person (or any service furnished by such person), and neither the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office nor the Agency shall be liable, as a result of such inclusion, for any portion of compensable injury, loss, or damage attributable to such person or service.

children.heading: Process for making reports
children.label: 1 Process for making reports
children.text: A person who elects to make a restricted report containing an allegation of sexual assault or sexual harassment shall submit the report to the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be treated by the person who receives the report in the same manner as a communication covered by the privilege set forth in this section;

children.label: B
children.text: shall not result in a referral to law enforcement or commencement of a formal administrative investigation, unless the victim elects to change the report from a restricted report to an unrestricted report;

children.label: C
children.text: in a case requiring an employee reassignment, relocation, or other mitigation or protective measures, shall result only in actions that are managed in a manner to limit, to the extent possible, the disclosure of any information contained in the report;

children.label: D
children.text: shall be exempt from any Federal or, to the maximum extent permitted by the Constitution, State reporting requirements, including the requirements under , , relevant provisions of Executive Order 12333 ( note; relating to United States intelligence activities), or successor order, Executive Order 13462 ( note; relating to President’s intelligence advisory board and intelligence oversight board), or successor order, title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ( et seq.), the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 ( et seq.), title I of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 ( et seq.), and sections 791 and 794a of title 29, except when reporting is necessary to prevent or mitigate an imminent threat of serious bodily harm.section 535(b) of title 28section 3517(b)(5) of this title50 U.S.C. 300150 U.S.C. 300142 U.S.C. 2000e29 U.S.C. 62142 U.S.C. 12111

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 3 Rule of construction
children.text: The receipt of a restricted report submitted under subsection (d) shall not be construed as imputing actual or constructive knowledge of an alleged incident of sexual assault or sexual harassment to the Agency for the purpose of the Agency’s responsibility to exercise reasonable care to take immediate and appropriate corrective action to prevent and correct harassing behavior.

children.heading: Privileged communications with Agency employees
children.label: e Privileged communications with Agency employees

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A victim shall be entitled to maintain and assert a privilege against disclosure of, and be able to prevent any other person from disclosing, any confidential communication made between the victim and any employee of the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office, if such communication was made for the purpose of facilitating advice or assistance to the victim in accordance with this section. A victim may consent to additional disclosures.

children.heading: When a communication is confidential
children.label: 2 When a communication is confidential
children.text: A communication is confidential for the purposes of this section if made in the course of the relationship between the victim and any employee of the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office and not intended to be disclosed to third persons, other than those to whom disclosure is made in furtherance of the provision of advice or assistance to the victim or those reasonably necessary for such transmission of the communication.

children.heading: Maintenance of privilege
children.label: 3 Maintenance of privilege
children.text: The privilege is maintained by the victim. A victim may authorize the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office employee who received the communication to assert the privilege on his or her behalf, with confidentiality. The Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office employee who received the communication may assert the privilege on behalf of the victim. The authority of such Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office employee to so assert the privilege is presumed in the absence of evidence to the contrary.

children.label: A
children.text: When the victim is deceased.

children.label: B
children.text: When the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office employee who received the communication has a reasonable belief that a victim’s mental or emotional condition makes the victim a danger to any person, including the victim.

children.label: C
children.text: When the otherwise privileged communication clearly contemplates the future commission of a crime or breach of national security, or aiding any individual to commit or plan to commit what the victim knew or reasonable should have known to be a crime or breach of national security.

children.label: D
children.text: When disclosure of a communication is constitutionally required.

children.heading: Handling of exceptions
children.label: 5 Handling of exceptions
children.text: When the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office employee determines that information requires an exception to the privilege, the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office employee who received the communication will protect information pertaining to the facts and circumstances surrounding the underlying sexual assault or sexual harassment allegations to the greatest extent possible.

children.heading: Incident reports when victim or alleged perpetrator is an Agency employee
children.label: f Incident reports when victim or alleged perpetrator is an Agency employee
A
children.text: the head of the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office is required to submit a written incident report not later than 8 days after receiving an unrestricted report containing an allegation of sexual assault or sexual harassment; and

children.label: i
children.text: the Director of the Agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Chief Operating Officer of the Agency;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Special Victim Investigator; and

children.label: iv
children.text: such other individuals as the Director determines appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: to record the details about actions taken or in progress to provide the necessary care and support to the victim of the alleged incident;

children.label: B
children.text: to document the referral of the allegations to the appropriate investigatory or law enforcement agency; and

children.label: C
children.text: to provide initial formal notification of the alleged incident.

children.label: A
children.text: The time, date, and location of the alleged sexual assault or sexual harassment.

children.label: B
children.text: An identification of the type of offense or harassment alleged.

children.label: C
children.text: An identification of the assigned office and location of the victim.

children.label: D
children.text: An identification of the assigned office and location of the alleged perpetrator, including information regarding whether the alleged perpetrator has been temporarily transferred or removed from an assignment or otherwise restricted, if applicable.

children.label: i
children.text: referral to any services available to victims, including the date of each referral;

children.label: ii
children.text: notification of the incident to appropriate investigatory organizations, including the organizations notified and dates of notifications; and

children.label: iii
children.text: issuance of any personal protection orders or steps taken to separate the victim and the alleged perpetrator within their place of employment.

children.label: F
children.text: Such other elements as the Director determines appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: The Director of the Agency.

children.label: B
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer of the Agency.

children.label: C
children.text: The Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office.

children.label: D
children.text: If the individual is an Agency employee, the head of the directorate employing the individual and the first-level supervisor of the individual.

children.label: E
children.text: If the individual is an Agency contractor, the Acquisition Group Chief and the contracting officer for the relevant contract. For industrial contractor personnel, the contracting officer shall notify the contractually identified representative for the prime contractor.

children.label: F
children.text: The Inspector General of the Agency.

children.label: G
children.text: Such other individuals as the Director determines appropriate.

children.heading: Restricted reports
children.label: 2 Restricted reports
children.text: In the case of restricted reports under subsection (d), the Sexual Harassment/Assault Response and Prevention Office shall notify any victims known to have filed a restricted report against an individual known to be the subject of at least one unrestricted allegation of sexual assault or sexual harassment by another reporter that another allegation has been made against the same individual who is the alleged subject of the victim’s report at the time of the victim’s initial report or any time thereafter upon receipt of any subsequent unrestricted report under subsection (c) or a common perpetrator notice under paragraph (1) of this subsection.
Any employee of the Agency.

Any person other than an Agency employee who alleges they were sexually assaulted or harassed at a facility associated with the Agency or during the performance of a function associated with the Agency.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall establish a system for the tracking and, in accordance with chapter 31 of title 44 (commonly known as the “Federal Records Act of 1950”), long-term temporary retention of all Agency records related to any investigation into an allegation of sexual assault or sexual harassment made in an unrestricted report, including any related medical documentation.

children.heading: Relation to privilege
children.label: 2 Relation to privilege
children.text: Any Agency records created under the authority of this section are subject to the privileges described in this section. Routine records management activities conducted by authorized Agency personnel with respect to such records, including maintaining, searching, or dispositioning of records, shall not result in a waiver of those privileges.

children.heading: Applicability to FOIA
children.label: 3 Applicability to FOIA
children.text: This section shall constitute a withholding statute pursuant to with respect to any information that may reveal the identity of a victim of sexual assault or sexual harassment, or any information subject to the privileges described in this section.section 552(b)(3) of title 5

children.heading: Relationship to the Office of Equal Employment Opportunity
children.label: j Relationship to the Office of Equal Employment Opportunity
children.text: In the case of a restricted report of sexual harassment, such report shall not result in a referral to the Office of Equal Employment Opportunity, unless the victim elects to change the report from a restricted report to an unrestricted report. In the case of an unrestricted report, the Special Victim Investigator, the Office of Equal Employment Opportunity, law enforcement, or any other appropriate investigative body, or any appropriate combination thereof, may investigate the unrestricted report, as appropriate. Policies and procedures developed pursuant to this section are intended to offer victims options in addition to the process described in part 1614 of title 29, Code of Federal Regulations, or successor regulations.

children.label: A
children.text: by a victim;

children.label: B
children.text: that describes information relating to an allegation of sexual assault or sexual harassment;

children.label: C
children.text: to an individual eligible to document an unrestricted or restricted report; and

children.label: D
children.text: that the victim intends to result in formal documentation of an unrestricted or restricted report.

children.heading: Victim
children.label: 2 Victim
children.text: The term “victim” means a person who alleges they have suffered direct physical or emotional harm because they were subjected to sexual assault or sexual harassment.
